growth_records.medium: Modified artificial seawater medium + 1 g/L yeast extract, sulfoquinovose (SQ) as added substrate

up.method: derived from measured % substrate consumption over time (Fig. 1f)

sec.units: concentration time-course in Fig. 1f (numeric values not in text)
concentration time-course in Fig. 1f (numeric values not in text)
sec.method: measured product accumulation (colorimetric Fuchsin assay, 580 nm; Fig. 1f,h)

growth_records.cite: Ma Xiaoyi et al. Cosmopolitan marine bacteria facilitate a vast phytoplankton-derived sulfonate-based carbon flow through sulfoquinovosidases. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-66898-z. [PMC12780224]
growth_records.method: reported (growth curve monitored via A600, Fig. 1f) but no numeric specific growth rate (µ) or doubling time stated in the paper
growth_records.snippet: We studied A. macleodii ATCC 27126 and demonstrated its ability to utilize SQ as a growth substrate. After 36 h of cultivation, the strain consumed approximately 64% of the SQ in the media, accompanied by the production of sulfate and sulfi
growth_records.notes: Not a GEM/MFA paper; enzyme-discovery/marine-biogeochem study with pure-culture feeding experiments. Uptake rate DERIVED: initial SQ = 333 µM; 64% consumed in 36 h. Consumed = 0.64 × 333 = 213.12 µM. Rate = 213.12 µM / 36 h = 5.92 µmol/L/h. This is an average VOLUMETRIC consumption rate over 36 h; no biomass (gDW) quantification is given, so mmol/gDW/h cannot be computed. No specific growth rate reported (growth curve only). SQ is catabolized via the sulfo-SDO pathway to sulfite + 6-dehydroglucose; sulfate/sulfite accumulation measured but not quantified in text (Source Data file / Fig. 1f). Base artificial-seawater salt composition not detailed in main text.

growth_records.medium: Modified artificial seawater medium + 1 g/L yeast extract, sulfoquinovosyl glycerol (SQGro) as added substrate

up.method: derived from measured % substrate consumption over time (Fig. 2b)

sec.units: measured accumulation (Fig. 2b); numeric values not in text
measured accumulation (Fig. 2b); numeric values not in text

growth_records.cite: Ma Xiaoyi et al. Cosmopolitan marine bacteria facilitate a vast phytoplankton-derived sulfonate-based carbon flow through sulfoquinovosidases. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-66898-z. [PMC12780224]
growth_records.method: reported (A600 growth curve, Fig. 2b) but no numeric µ or doubling time stated
growth_records.snippet: Growth experiments conducted with A. macleodii ATCC 27126 showed that it consumed 77% of SQGro within 36 h (Fig. 2b), producing sulfite and sulfate, suggesting it contains an SQase capable of cleaving SQGro to give SQ, which is metabolized
growth_records.notes: Uptake rate DERIVED: initial SQGro = 222 µM; 77% consumed in 36 h. Consumed = 0.77 × 222 = 170.94 µM. Rate = 170.94 µM / 36 h = 4.75 µmol/L/h (average volumetric). No biomass quantification, so no gDW normalization; no numeric growth rate reported. SQase (AmSqgH, GH31) cleaves SQGro to SQ; SQ then catabolized via sulfo-SDO to sulfite. Base artificial-seawater salt composition not detailed in main text.
